{
  "cells": [
    {
      "cell_type": "markdown",
      "metadata": {},
      "source": [
        "---\n",
        "title: \"Crossover trial design simulations\"\n",
        "author: \"Saumil Shah\"\n",
        "date: \"May 9, 2025\"\n",
        "\n",
        "format:\n",
        "  html:\n",
        "    theme: brite\n",
        "    toc: true\n",
        "    code-fold: false\n",
        "from: markdown+emoji\n",
        "---\n",
        "\n",
        "::: callout-tip\n",
        "This document follows a notation <kbd>file-names.R</kbd>, *{Rpackage}* names, or `variable_names` to disambiguate. Please reach out to [Saumil](mailto:saumil.shah@boehringer-ingelheim.com) for your suggestions to this template.\n",
        ":::\n",
        "\n",
        "::: callout-tip\n",
        "For help with setting up access to tools, and guides on how to use them [go here!](https://boehringer.sharepoint.com/:f:/s/z365tamstat/Ehh42KsNctpFnqgE77hqDy8BcorXNEVYkQ_AIG7xgiseLQ?e=r8Arar)\n",
        ":::\n",
        "\n",
        "\n",
        "## Introduction\n",
        "\n",
        "Think of this template as a form for simulations in R. Arrange your simulations in the following sequence of steps:\n",
        "\n",
        "1.  Packages\n",
        "2.  Scenarios\n",
        "3.  Priors (optional)\n",
        "4.  Data generation\n",
        "5.  Analysis\n",
        "6.  Summary\n",
        "\n",
        "This document follows the same sequence. Use it as a form to fill out the details of your simulations. If this document is getting too long feel free to define your functions in other files, for example: <kbd>4-data-generation.R</kbd>, and then source them in the appropriate code blocks using\n",
        "\n",
        "```         \n",
        "source(\"4-data-generation.R\")\n",
        "```\n",
        "\n",
        "::: callout-important\n",
        "Some code blocks in this document has specific variables. These variables are essential for functioning of this template. Unless you are a power user and know exactly what you are doing **please do not rename, or remove them!** For ease of identification look for a comment block starting with `[!]`.\n",
        ":::\n",
        "\n",
        "## 1. Packages\n",
        "\n",
        "Please add all R packages to `packages` vector that your simulation depends on.\n",
        "\n",
        "```{r add packages}\n",
        "# list of packages used by the simulation\n",
        "# [!] packages\n",
        "packages <- c(\n",
        "  \"rstatix\",\n",
        "  \"caracas\",\n",
        "  \"tidyverse\"\n",
        ")\n",
        "```\n",
        "\n",
        "::: callout-tip\n",
        "This document a. provides an overview of the details of the simulations, and b. helps run the simulations. To hide a section from the rendered overview add `{.hidden}` in the section title. Check this file in Rstudio source/visual mode to see these hidden sections.\n",
        ":::\n",
        "\n",
        "### Load packages {.hidden}\n",
        "\n",
        "We must load this packages for the rest of the document to function. Please run the following code block unchanged.\n",
        "\n",
        "```{r load packages}\n",
        "# load / require each package\n",
        "sapply(packages, require, character.only=TRUE)\n",
        "```\n",
        "\n",
        "## 2. Scenarios\n",
        "\n",
        "The rows of `scenarios` tibble will feature each scenario that we want to simulate. Scenarios can be anything that we want to tryout for the ideation. For crossover design, we will try sets of sequences, with and without carryover effects.\n",
        "\n",
        "A crossover design can be shown as a design table of sequences and periods. For example,\n",
        "\n",
        "::::: columns\n",
        "::: {.column width=\"25%\"}\n",
        ":::\n",
        "\n",
        "::: {.column width=\"50%\"}\n",
        "| Period/Sequence | Sequence.1 | Sequence.2 |\n",
        "|-----------------|------------|------------|\n",
        "| **Period.1**    | A          | B          |\n",
        "| **Period.2**    | B          | A          |\n",
        "\n",
        ": Design table for the AB \\| BA design.\n",
        ":::\n",
        ":::::\n",
        "\n",
        "### Crossover designs\n",
        "\n",
        "We want to test two designs AB \\| BA, and ABBA \\| BAAB \\| AABB \\| BBAA with and without carryover effects. For this simulation we will only focus on first-order carry over effects.\n",
        "\n",
        "```{r add scenarios}\n",
        "# each tibble is a design consisting of set of sequences\n",
        "designs <- list(\n",
        "  tibble(seq = c(\"AB\", \"BA\")),\n",
        "  tibble(seq = c(\"ABBA\", \"BAAB\", \"AABB\", \"BBAA\"))\n",
        ")\n",
        "\n",
        "carryover <- c(FALSE, TRUE)\n",
        "```\n",
        "\n",
        "### Endpoint distributions\n",
        "\n",
        "Let us define the parameters for the endpoint. We will assume that the endpoint is continuous and follows Normal distribution.\n",
        "\n",
        "```{r true difference}\n",
        "# true treatment difference in each design\n",
        "true.diff <- c(1,1)\n",
        "```\n",
        "\n",
        "#### Mean\n",
        "\n",
        "First we define endpoint mean in the same order as the designs. Ensure that tibble column names match the sequences as is. The first two are AB \\| BA designs without and with carryover. The next two are ABBA \\| BAAB \\| AABB \\| BBAA without and with carryover effects. We assume that treatment A has carryover effect of `-0.1`, and treatment B `+0.2`.\n",
        "\n",
        "```{r endpoint mean}\n",
        "endpoint.mu <- list(\n",
        "  tibble(\n",
        "    AB = c(1, 2),\n",
        "    BA = c(2, 1)\n",
        "  ),\n",
        "  tibble(\n",
        "    AB = c(1, 1.9),\n",
        "    BA = c(2, 1.2)\n",
        "  ),\n",
        "  tibble(\n",
        "    ABBA = c(1, 2, 2, 1),\n",
        "    BAAB = c(2, 1, 1, 2),\n",
        "    AABB = c(1, 1, 2, 2),\n",
        "    BBAA = c(2, 2, 1, 1)\n",
        "  ),\n",
        "  tibble(\n",
        "    ABBA = c(1, 1.9, 2.2, 1.2),\n",
        "    BAAB = c(2, 1.2, 0.9, 1.9),\n",
        "    AABB = c(1, 0.9, 1.9, 2.2),\n",
        "    BBAA = c(2, 2.2, 1.2, 0.9)\n",
        "  )\n",
        ")\n",
        "```\n",
        "\n",
        "#### Standard deviation\n",
        "\n",
        "I will repeat a similar process for standard deviation of the endpoint.\n",
        "\n",
        "```{r endpoint sd}\n",
        "endpoint.sd <- list(\n",
        "  tibble(\n",
        "    AB = c(0.25, 0.35),\n",
        "    BA = c(0.35, 0.25)\n",
        "  ),\n",
        "  tibble(\n",
        "    AB = c(0.25, 0.33),\n",
        "    BA = c(0.35, 0.27)\n",
        "  ),\n",
        "  tibble(\n",
        "    ABBA = c(0.25, 0.35, 0.35, 0.25),\n",
        "    BAAB = c(0.35, 0.25, 0.25, 0.35),\n",
        "    AABB = c(0.25, 0.25, 0.35, 0.35),\n",
        "    BBAA = c(0.35, 0.35, 0.25, 0.25)\n",
        "  ),\n",
        "  tibble(\n",
        "    ABBA = c(0.25, 0.33, 0.35, 0.27),\n",
        "    BAAB = c(0.35, 0.27, 0.25, 0.33),\n",
        "    AABB = c(0.25, 0.25, 0.33, 0.35),\n",
        "    BBAA = c(0.35, 0.35, 0.27, 0.25)\n",
        "  )\n",
        ")\n",
        "```\n",
        "\n",
        "### Sample size\n",
        "\n",
        "We want to try out two sample sizes per sequence.\n",
        "\n",
        "```{r sample size}\n",
        "sample.size <- c(24,36)\n",
        "```\n",
        "\n",
        "### Get scenarios\n",
        "\n",
        "Now we have all the raw material to generate a tibble of scenarios. We will use `get_scenarios()` function for this. Please check <kbd>R/1-scenarios.R</kbd> for the implementation.\n",
        "\n",
        "_Please run this block unchanged!_\n",
        "\n",
        "::: callout-tip\n",
        "> _Please run this block unchanged!_\n",
        "\n",
        "This is to retain basic functionality of the template. If you know what you are doing feel free to change it to your needs.\n",
        ":::\n",
        "\n",
        "```{r get scenarios}\n",
        "source(\"R/1-scenarios.R\")\n",
        "\n",
        "# [!] scenarios\n",
        "scenarios <- get_scenarios(design, endpoint.mu, endpoint.sd, sample.size)\n",
        "# this is for prettier printing\n",
        "scenarios |> rowwise() |>\n",
        "    mutate(\n",
        "      design = gsub(\" \", \" \\\\| \", do.call(paste, as.list(design$seq))),\n",
        "      mu = do.call(paste, c(as.list(do.call(paste, mu)), sep=\" | \")),\n",
        "      sd = do.call(paste, c(as.list(do.call(paste, sd)), sep=\" | \"))\n",
        "    )\n",
        "```\n",
        "\n",
        "### Expand scenarios {.hidden}\n",
        "\n",
        "Unique aspect of crossover design is that each sequence can be thought of an independent experiment. We will use this fact to further chop and parallelize our simulations. Don't worry, at the time of analysis we will use the sequences that belong to one trial together. Please run this block unchanged!\n",
        "\n",
        "```{r expand scenarios}\n",
        "expand_scenarios <- function(scenarios) {\n",
        "  expand_scenarios <- scenarios |>\n",
        "    rowwise() |>\n",
        "    # figure out how many sequences are there\n",
        "    mutate(seq.id = list(as.list(seq(1, nrow(design))))) |>\n",
        "    # each seq.id gets new line\n",
        "    unnest_longer(seq.id) |>\n",
        "    # write out sequences explicitly\n",
        "    rowwise() |>\n",
        "    mutate(seq = design$seq[seq.id]) |>\n",
        "    # infer periods\n",
        "    rowwise() |>\n",
        "    mutate(period = list(as.list(seq(1, nchar(seq))))) |>\n",
        "    # each period gets a new line\n",
        "    unnest_longer(period) |>\n",
        "    # infer the treatment\n",
        "    rowwise() |>\n",
        "    mutate(treatment = unlist(strsplit(seq, split = \"\"))[period]) |>\n",
        "    # infer mu and sd\n",
        "    rowwise() |>\n",
        "    mutate(mu = mu[[period, seq]], sd = sd[[period, seq]])\n",
        "}\n",
        "\n",
        "# [!] expanded_scenarios\n",
        "expanded_scenarios <- expand_scenarios(scenarios)\n",
        "```\n",
        "\n",
        "## 3. Priors (optional) {.hidden}\n",
        "\n",
        "We do not have any real world data to include at the moment.\n",
        "\n",
        "## 4. Data generation\n",
        "\n",
        "We may use any variables (tibble columns) we defined in scenarios (expanded scenarios). Let's use `sample.size`, `mu`, `sd`, and `seq.id` for this function and sample endpoint using `rnorm()`. One trial subject goes through all the blocks within one sequence. Each sequence has a unique set of subjects.\n",
        "\n",
        "```{r get data}\n",
        "# function to generate endpoint data for a block\n",
        "# [!] get_data\n",
        "get_data <- function(\n",
        "    sample.size,\n",
        "    mu,\n",
        "    sd,\n",
        "    seq.id\n",
        ") {\n",
        "\n",
        "  tibble(\n",
        "    # [!] patient\n",
        "    patient = 1:sample.size + (seq.id-1)*sample.size,\n",
        "    # [!] endpoint\n",
        "    endpoint = rnorm(sample.size, mu, sd)\n",
        "  )\n",
        "\n",
        "}\n",
        "```\n",
        "\n",
        "## 5. Analysis\n",
        "\n",
        "There are three analyses in this project:\n",
        "\n",
        "1.  Bias of the crossover design\n",
        "2.  Paired Wilcoxon tests for differences in treatment effects\n",
        "3.  Linear mixed effects modeling to estimate treatment differences\n",
        "\n",
        "Please check <kbd>R/3-algebra.R</kbd>, and <kbd>R/4-analysis.R</kbd> for the implementation.\n",
        "\n",
        "```{r analysis, output=FALSE}\n",
        "source(\"R/3-algebra.R\")\n",
        "source(\"R/4-analysis.R\")\n",
        "```\n",
        "\n",
        "```{r show analysis, eval=FALSE, echo=FALSE, output=FALSE, purl=FALSE}\n",
        "get_analysis\n",
        "```\n",
        "\n",
        "## 6. Summary\n",
        "\n",
        "We summarize the difference in treatments, and how well each design performed. Please check <kbd>R/5-summary.R</kbd> for the implementation.\n",
        "\n",
        "```{r summary, output=FALSE}\n",
        "source(\"R/5-summary.R\")\n",
        "```\n",
        "\n",
        "```{r show summary, eval=FALSE, echo=FALSE, output=FALSE, purl=FALSE}\n",
        "get_summary\n",
        "```\n",
        "\n",
        "## Run simulations\n",
        "\n",
        "We will collect everything defined in here. Process it and run the simulations. First we define how many replicates do we want.\n",
        "\n",
        "```{r total reps}\n",
        "total_reps <- 200\n",
        "```\n",
        "\n",
        "Then finally we run profiling, and the simulations.\n",
        "\n",
        "```{r extract code, eval=FALSE, output=FALSE, purl=FALSE}\n",
        "source(\"simulate.R\")\n",
        "```\n",
        "\n",
        "## Results\n",
        "\n",
        "We can see that the balanced design without any bias is better at infering the treatment differences.\n",
        "\n",
        "Symbol legend:\n",
        "\n",
        "  - `p` period fixed effect, example: `p1` is period 1\n",
        "  - `q` sequence fixed effect, example: `q2` is sequence 2\n",
        "  - `r` carryover fixed effect, example: `rA` is carryover of treatment B\n",
        "\n",
        "```{r show results, purl=FALSE}\n",
        "targets::tar_load(tbl_summary)\n",
        "tbl_summary\n",
        "```\n",
        "\n",
        "## Plots\n",
        "\n",
        "```{r show plots, purl=FALSE}\n",
        "targets::tar_load(plots)\n",
        "plots\n",
        "```"
      ]
    }
  ],
  "metadata": {
    "kernelspec": {
      "name": "python3",
      "language": "python",
      "display_name": "Python 3 (ipykernel)",
      "path": "/opt/python/3.13.5/share/jupyter/kernels/python3"
    }
  },
  "nbformat": 4,
  "nbformat_minor": 4
}
